bullets.text: French MPs have approved a new assisted dying law, allowing terminally ill adults to seek assisted dying under strict criteria after years of debate.
A new Pew study suggests that more people globally now favor China over the US, with greater confidence expressed in Xi Jinping compared to Donald Trump.
A US national who contracted Ebola has arrived in Germany for treatment, as the Trump administration bars Americans from traveling to the US on commercial flights if they have the virus.
Former employees of the charity PAWS Darwin are speaking out about being short-changed on their superannuation, with an estimated $150,000 owed.
Adelaide University researchers have developed new laser technology that can test closed alcohol bottles for methanol, a substance responsible for hundreds of deaths annually.
Brisbane's nightlife entertainment sector is experiencing a surge, with a new report indicating the city has more after-hours venues than Sydney or Melbourne.

Oil traders are warning that the market is nearing depletion as global stockpiles, which previously absorbed shocks from the Iran war, are running low due to the closure of the crucial Strait of Hormuz.
China's trade gap is narrowing, indicating fiscal austerity within the world's second-biggest economy, even as the country faces challenges in funding its ambitious technology development goals.

Storm clouds are gathering over America's financial supremacy, with its payments firms potentially being the first casualties, while the liquefied natural gas (LNG) market could face disruptions from war, weather, and outages before winter.
